final_answer:
Implemented: I finished the path/file rename pass and kept the bridge buildable.

Why it matters: the bridge tree now matches Gilt naming at directory/file level instead of mixed legacy paths.

Facts (confirmed):
- Top-level clients are now [bridge/gilt-exec](/srv/repos/gold-chain/bridge/gilt-exec) and [bridge/gilt-consensus](/srv/repos/gold-chain/bridge/gilt-consensus) (old `bridge/bor` and `bridge/heimdall-v2` paths removed from bridge code paths).
- Key internal path renames are applied and compiling (for example `consensus/bor -> consensus/gilt`, `x/bor -> x/gilt`, `api/proto .../heimdallv2 -> .../giltconsensusv2`).
- Solidity child path rename is applied: [ChildChain.sol](/srv/repos/gold-chain/bridge/pos-contracts/contracts/child/ChildChain.sol), [ChildERC20.sol](/srv/repos/gold-chain/bridge/pos-contracts/contracts/child/ChildERC20.sol), [ChildERC721.sol](/srv/repos/gold-chain/bridge/pos-contracts/contracts/child/ChildERC721.sol) now import `./gilt/...`.
- File/path scan in `bridge/` (excluding deps/vendor/node_modules/build) shows no remaining legacy `bridge/bor`, `bridge/heimdall-v2`, `testnet-amoy`, `config/amoy`, `tests/bor`, `packaging/deb/bor` paths.
- Checks passed:
  - `make build` in [bridge/gilt-consensus](/srv/repos/gold-chain/bridge/gilt-consensus)
  - `make bor` in [bridge/gilt-exec](/srv/repos/gold-chain/bridge/gilt-exec)
  - `go test -run TestDoesNotExist ./x/gilt/... ./bridge/...` in `gilt-consensus`
  - `go test -run TestDoesNotExist ./consensus/gilt/... ./miner ./eth/... ./tests/gilt/...` in `gilt-exec`
  - `npx hardhat compile` in [bridge/pos-contracts](/srv/repos/gold-chain/bridge/pos-contracts)

Inference:
- The remaining legacy terms are primarily textual protocol/content mentions, not file/path naming blockers.

Actions (next, if you want full purge):
1. Run a final text-token purge for remaining `bor/heimdall` content strings (API paths, comments, docs, telemetry labels) and update compatibility assumptions where required.
2. Re-run full bridge test matrix after that purge.

Completion level: partial for full text-token purge; complete for file/path rename + buildability.
